newsletters.title: My Editor Caught Me Sounding Like AI. Now AI Catches Me First.
newsletters.content:
Turning a list of my writing foibles into a skill that stops me from getting away with them  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTXkgRWRpdG9yIENhdWdodCBNZSBTb3VuZGluZyBMaWtlIEFJLiBOb3cgQUkgQ2F0Y2hlcyBNZSBGaXJzdC4iLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjcwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MH0=

Working Overtime

https://every.


MY EDITOR CAUGHT ME SOUNDING LIKE AI. NOW AI CATCHES ME FIRST. https://every.


TURNING A LIST OF MY WRITING FOIBLES INTO A SKILL THAT STOPS ME FROM GETTING AWAY WITH THEM https://every.

by Katie Parrott https://every.

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTXkgRWRpdG9yIENhdWdodCBNZSBTb3VuZGluZyBMaWtlIEFJLiBOb3cgQUkgQ2F0Y2hlcyBNZSBGaXJzdC4iLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjcwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9hY2NvdW50IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjV9 to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



Before a recent one-on-one with Kate Lee https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTXkgRWRpdG9yIENhdWdodCBNZSBTb3VuZGluZyBMaWtlIEFJLiBOb3cgQUkgQ2F0Y2hlcyBNZSBGaXJzdC4iLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjcwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9Aa2F0ZV8xNzY3IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9, Every’s editor in chief, I opened our shared document and found a list of my own writing fails staring back at me. My drafts had picked up too many of the AI tells that both I—and you—know how to spot from across the room: the symmetrical sentence structures, the little rhetorical throat-clears, the phrases that sound profound on first pass but on closer inspection contain nothing but air, and those pesky sets of three.

The worst part was that I should know better. I am the person at Every who writes about writing with AI while using AI to write about writing with AI. I have custom agents, style guides https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTXkgRWRpdG9yIENhdWdodCBNZSBTb3VuZGluZyBMaWtlIEFJLiBOb3cgQUkgQ2F0Y2hlcyBNZSBGaXJzdC4iLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjcwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9ndWlkZXMvYWktc3R5bGUtZ3VpZGU_c291cmNlPXBvc3RfYnV0dG9uIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9, editorial workflows, and an apparently bottomless appetite for turning every lesson into a system. And still, I had let the machine’s smoothness https://every. pass for my own judgment enough times that my editors felt the need to intervene.

After the meeting, I did what I generally do when I learn something new, embarrassing or otherwise: I baked it into documentation for my agents. I opened the notes, pulled out the patterns Kate had flagged, and listed them in a new skill called /guardrails, which turns any agent I write with into an exacting editorial specialist that keeps me honest.

I’ll never be completely done with /guardrails, or any of the review skills like it that I’ve built, because my human tics and tendencies will move around like a squirmy toddler. But I’d rather make new mistakes than keep repeating the old ones. Review skills are the mechanism by which I do that. They’re another form of editor, one that can catch a draft’s more annoying weak spots before they become a human editor’s problem.

https://every. start of my guardrails skill, where I’ve compiled all the particular ways that content I submit can fall below par. (All images courtesy of Katie Parrott.)




Writing with AI tends to be portrayed as a bargain: The machine does more, so the human does less. But in my experience—a microcosm of Every CEO Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTXkgRWRpdG9yIENhdWdodCBNZSBTb3VuZGluZyBMaWtlIEFJLiBOb3cgQUkgQ2F0Y2hlcyBNZSBGaXJzdC4iLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjcwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AZGFuc2hpcHBlciIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMH0=’s argument in “After Automation” https://every.—it changes what the human does instead of reducing the workload. I have to be clear about defining my standards so a model can understand them. That creates more work—but it helps me understand them better myself https://every..

Setting up reviews like /guardrails takes time, attention, and a certain comfort with a tool like Codex https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiTXkgRWRpdG9yIENhdWdodCBNZSBTb3VuZGluZyBMaWtlIEFJLiBOb3cgQUkgQ2F0Y2hlcyBNZSBGaXJzdC4iLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjcwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiO

newsletters.title: AI Is Ready. Organizations Aren’t.
newsletters.content:
Plus: Spiral 4.0 writes in your voice, and why the next blockbuster drug may come from China  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjB9


AI IS READY. ORGANIZATIONS AREN’T. https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcC9haS1pcy1yZWFkeS1vcmdhbml6YXRpb25zLWFyZW4tdCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxfQ==


PLUS: SPIRAL 4.0 WRITES IN YOUR VOICE, AND WHY THE NEXT BLOCKBUSTER DRUG MAY COME FROM CHINA https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcC9haS1pcy1yZWFkeS1vcmdhbml6YXRpb25zLWFyZW4tdCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoyfQ==

by Every Staff https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQEV2ZXJ5JTIwU3RhZmYiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Hello, and happy Sunday! This week the consulting team published two practical guides. Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG1pa2VfMjExNCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo0fQ== built on engineer Steve Yegge’s viral post to map the eight levels of AI adoption https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZ3VpZGVzL3RoZS1laWdodC1sZXZlbHMtb2YtYWktYWRvcHRpb24iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0=—with sample prompts and signals for when to move up—and Natalia Quintero https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG5hdGFsaWFfMjk0NCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo2fQ== (who’s talked to leadership teams https://every. at hundreds of organizations) laid out a foolproof five-step process https://every. for executives rolling out AI across their companies. Covering Microsoft Build https://every., Mike argued that enterprise adoption https://every. lags the news cycle—a gap he sees up close with the enterprise clients he advises. He also made a counterargument to Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGRhbnNoaXBwZXIiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTF9’s essay about the future of work, “After Automation.” https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vcC9hZnRlci1hdXRvbWF0aW9uIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEyfQ==

Spiral https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vb24tZXZlcnkvc3BpcmFsLTQtMC1nb2VzLWFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxM30= 4.0 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vb24tZXZlcnkvc3BpcmFsLTQtMC1nb2VzLWFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNH0= shipped this week: Every’s writing tool can now draft in your voice from inside any agent, with a price cut to match. Elsewhere, Figma’s Matt Colyer makes the case that the SaaSpocalypse https://every. is overblown, designer Daniel Rodrigues https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGRhbmllbF81ZmJkMjFfMSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNn0= shares a two-tool image generators https://every. workflow, Monologue https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vd3d3Lm1vbm9sb2d1ZS50by8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTh9 general manager Naveen Naidu https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgSXMgUmVhZHkuIE9yZ2FuaXphdGlvbnMgQXJlbuKAmXQuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDI5MSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG5hdmVlbl82ODA0IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE5fQ== has a system for making coding agents https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJ

newsletters.title: AI is starting to build itself
newsletters.content:
~[Sign up](https://www.theaivalley.com/)~~ | ~~[Follow us on X](https://twitter.com/heyBarsee)~~ | ~~[Sponsor](mailto:barsee@aivalley.ai)~

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/c167fb98-39eb-42d4-a114-56fe02753cac/2__1_-min__2_-min.png?t=1744033912)
Follow image link: (https://www.theaivalley.com/subscribe)
Caption: 

Together with

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/66a418ac-e238-4410-8a67-1d86670433eb/unknown__1_.png?t=1780669489)
Follow image link: (https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)
Caption: 



----------
==howdy, it’s Barsee again.==

==happy friday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.==

==**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**==

* Anthropic says Claude is already helping build Claude

* ChatGPT can now show you everything it remembers

* Zuckerberg wants Meta’s AI agents to run entire businesses

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **REMIO**

## [The #1 Agent with all your context](https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/811c5f21-e33b-4a61-b19b-aff2253362f9/helloworld44__1_.png?t=1780669470)
Follow image link: (https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)
Caption: Courtesy: Remio

No more copy-pasting. No more starting from scratch.

Just tell remio what you need — it already knows the rest. Write docs, generate reports, draft emails, create slides, all powered by everything you've ever worked on.

Your context. Your agent. Always ready.

[Try it here](https://www.remio.ai/?utm_source=aivalley)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

##### 1/ Anthropic says Claude is already helping build Claude

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/133e4875-b7e8-423c-ac63-a585cf17e6ec/Screenshot_2026-06-05_at_11.47.49_AM__1_.png?t=1780669748)
Caption: 

Anthropic has published a [new report](https://www.anthropic.com/institute/recursive-self-improvement) exploring recursive self-improving AI, the idea that AI systems could eventually help build more capable versions of themselves. The company revealed that more than 80% of the code merged into its production systems in May was written by Claude, while engineers now ship roughly eight times more code per day than they did in 2024. Anthropic says Claude is increasingly being used to write code, run experiments, and tackle open-ended engineering problems. While the company says fully self-improving AI is not guaranteed, it believes the possibility is becoming harder to dismiss. As Anthropic puts it, Claude is no longer just helping people build AI. It's starting to help build itself.

##### 2/ ChatGPT can now show you everything it remembers

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/bf9663a8-341b-4396-9eb3-556a5ae9324a/memorydreaming__1_.png?t=1780669760)
Caption: 

OpenAI has launched a major upgrade to ChatGPT’s memory system, giving users a [dedicated page](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-memory-dreaming) where they can see, edit, and remove what the chatbot remembers about them. Instead of storing isolated facts, ChatGPT now builds a running profile from past conversations, including preferences, interests, and recurring topics. Users can also tell ChatGPT what to remember and what to forget. OpenAI says the update has nearly doubled memory accuracy in internal testing. The company believes future AI assistants won't start from scratch every conversation. They'll gradually learn how each person prefers to work, communicate, and get things done.

##### 3/ Zuckerberg wants Meta’s AI agents to run entire businesses

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/49a71be3-7939-44aa-ad09-8f53ab35c355/Zuckerburg_No_2520Text__1_.png?t=1780669695)
Caption: 

Meta has launched a new AI agent across WhatsApp, Instagram, and Messenger that can answer customer questions, book appointments, and help close sales. The company says [future versions](https://www.wsj.com/tech/mark-zuckerberg-wants-metas-new-ai-agents-to-run-your-whole-business-6e2100e2?st=BX2Y2i&reflink=desktopwebshare_permalink&mod=tldr&utm_source=tldrnewsletter) will be able to conduct market research, analyze competitors, and connect with business tools like calendars and scheduling systems. More than one million businesses are already using the agent, which is currently free but will eventually become a paid product. Zuckerberg says the long-term goal is much bigger: Meta wants AI agents to handle more and more of a company's daily operations. For Meta, the opportunity extends far beyond social media, with more than 200 million businesses already using WhatsApp to communicate with customers.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Ideogram 4.0](https://x.com/ideogram_ai/status/2062202208700313872?s=20) > the world's best open image model

* [ChatGPT Dreaming](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-memory-dreaming) > OpenAI rolls out a more capable memory synthesis system for Plus and Pro users in the US

* [Reve 2.0](https://x.com/reve/status/2062260665121919101?s=20) > the world's best 4K image model

* [Google Dreambeans](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-labs/dreambeans) > an app that connects you with what matters

* [Vokal](https://vokal.team/) > a shared workspace where humans and AI agents collaborate without copy-paste handoffs

* [Miso One](https://x.com/AodenTeoMT/status/2062204362102100295?s=20) > the world's most expressive voice model

* [Magenta RealTime 2](https://x.com/googlegemma/status/2062619217967628693?s=20) > a new open model musicians can play like an instrument

* [Android Fake Call Detection](https://x.com/Google/status/2062192586891174170) > warns users when it detects a potential impersonator


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------
* [Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky plans new AI lab](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-06-04/airbnb-ceo-brian-chesky-plans-to-start-a-new-ai-company)

* [Bots have now passed human traffic online](https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/artificial-intelligence/bots-have-now-passed-human-traffic-online-cloudflare-boss-laments-says-agentic-traffic-wasnt-expected-to-eclipse-real-people-until-next-year?utm_source=tldrnewsletter)

* [China is training a robot future, one folded shirt at a time](https://restofworld.org/2026/china-ai-robotics-training-data)


----------

----------
# THE VALLEY GEMS

newsletters.title: How Microsoft Is Building for a World of Metered Intelligence
newsletters.content:
The company gets pragmatic in AI’s expensive era  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IE1pY3Jvc29mdCBJcyBCdWlsZGluZyBmb3IgYSBXb3JsZCBvZiBNZXRlcmVkIEludGVsbGlnZW5jZSAiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjkwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MH0=

Also True for Humans

https://every.


HOW MICROSOFT IS BUILDING FOR A WORLD OF METERED INTELLIGENCE https://every.


THE COMPANY GETS PRAGMATIC IN AI’S EXPENSIVE ERA https://every.

by Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IE1pY3Jvc29mdCBJcyBCdWlsZGluZyBmb3IgYSBXb3JsZCBvZiBNZXRlcmVkIEludGVsbGlnZW5jZSAiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjkwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AbWlrZV8yMTE0IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IE1pY3Jvc29mdCBJcyBCdWlsZGluZyBmb3IgYSBXb3JsZCBvZiBNZXRlcmVkIEludGVsbGlnZW5jZSAiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0MjkwLCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9hY2NvdW50IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjV9 to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



As I rode in my Uber to Microsoft’s annual Build conference on Monday, I fondly recalled a time when you could get anywhere in San Francisco for $5. Those days are long gone. Venture capitalists lost their appetite to supply unlimited funding in a viciously competitive market, and Uber needed to show a path to profitability ahead of its 2019 IPO.

There are signs that the “$5 Uber era” of LLMs is over now, too. AI labs are subsidizing subscriptions to the tune of thousands of dollars https://every., which can’t continue forever. This year Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI are all going public—and like Uber seven years ago, they’ll need to take a hard look at their books. On June 1, the eve of the event, Microsoft sparked outrage by switching to token-based billing on GitHub Copilot. Some users said their bills jumped from $39 to over $3,000 https://every. per month https://every..

Rather than backtracking on billing, Microsoft used the conference stage in California to make the case for using AI more pragmatically in the face of rising costs. I came away from the event thinking that Microsoft is the first company to get real about a world where intelligence is available on tap, but constrained by how many coins you can put in the meter. Here is what the company’s vision looks like in practice, and what it might tell us about how we’ll be paying for and pricing AI in the future.


INTELLIGENCE ON AND OFF THE METER: A PRODUCT APPROACH

In his opening speech, CEO Satya Nadella addressed pricing concerns head-on. He promised “unmetered intelligence to every desk and every home,” an AI-era update to Bill Gates https://every.’s vision https://every. of “a computer on every desk.”

https://every. CEO Satya Nadella promised “unmetered intelligence to every desk and every home.” (All images courtesy of Mike Taylor.)




The most tangible way to experience that vision is with the RTX Spark, a new laptop Microsoft designed for AI workloads with Nvidia. The device is able to run a medium-sized 128-billion-parameter model locally (frontier models are in the trillions of parameters) so developers can get a lot of work done without paying a penny for tokens. Microsoft is taking advantage of the fact that the leading open-source models like Kimi-K2.6 https://every., which have a trillion parameters, are too big to fit on most laptops, and is betting that budget-conscious coders might not mind being a year or two behind the frontier and use a smaller model. The device will be released in the fall.

The RTX Spark laptop follows earlier feature annou

newsletters.content:
Write more like yourself wherever you work  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

On Every

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL29uLWV2ZXJ5IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjF9


SPIRAL 4.0 GOES AGENT-NATIVE https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL29uLWV2ZXJ5L3NwaXJhbC00LTAtZ29lcy1hZ2VudC1uYXRpdmUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Mn0=


WRITE MORE LIKE YOURSELF WHEREVER YOU WORK https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL29uLWV2ZXJ5L3NwaXJhbC00LTAtZ29lcy1hZ2VudC1uYXRpdmUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

by Marcus Moretti https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BtYXJjdXNfZmQ4MzAyXzEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

Figma/Midjourney/Every illustration.

TL;DR: Spiral https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3dyaXRld2l0aHNwaXJhbC5jb20vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjV9 v4 just shipped with four major updates: a style engine that generates writing indistinguishable from your own 87 percent of the time, agent-native access via MCP, CLI, and API, team workspaces for writing in a shared voice, and a $10 price drop, bringing personal plans to start at $15 a month. Spiral will continue to be free for paid Every subscribers https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3N1YnNjcmliZSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo2fQ== along with access to all our tools and content.

Try Spiral 4.0 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3dyaXRld2l0aHNwaXJhbC5jb20vP3NvdXJjZT1wb3N0X2J1dHRvbiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo3fQ==



----------------------------------------



Today we’re announcing a number of updates to Spiral, the writing partner for you and your agent. Spiral is built by writers for writers, to help you from idea to line edit, matching your writing style throughout.

THE HIGHLIGHTS:

 * With stylometry https://every. (or the study of writing styles), Spiral now sounds more like you. We’ve built a new Style Engine from the ground up, so Spiral computes your writing fingerprint and picks relevant samples for new drafts.
 * Use Spiral wherever you do work. With a new MCP, plus our existing CLI and API, Spiral can step in if you’re underwhelmed by your agent’s writing output, or need good writing in any workflow.
 * For teams, use Spiral to speak with one voice. Team workspaces let you share styles, prompts, knowledge, and now chats and drafts.
 * And finally, we’ve given Spiral a new coat of paint and logo, designed by Daniel Rodrigues https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BkYW5pZWxfNWZiZDIxXzEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0=. The primary brand font is now Edgar, from Frere-Jones Type.

Since re-launching https://every. at the end of last year, Spiral has:

 * Created 5,524 from 168,464 writing samples
 * Generated 113,165 drafts
 * Made 350,078 revisions

It also now averages a 4.9/5 conversation score on our internal LLM-as-judge eval.

We built Spiral to help people who write for work write better. Just as Cursor is a coding harness, Spiral is a writing harness, supporting you at every stage of the writing process. Here’s how:

 1. Before you start writing, Spiral vets the clarity of your idea and materials to substantiate it. From basic writing prompts to the hard-won insights from Every’s editorial and social media teams, multiple 12,000-word system prompts govern Spiral’s workflow. (To get the style and substance just right, we’ve iterated on these system prompts 131 times so far.)
 2. When it’s time to draft, Spiral uses stylometry to reproduce your voice, working in Every’s know-how where appropriate. For example, if you ask Spiral for tweets, it will incorporate best practices from X’s latest algorithm update.
 3. When you need help polishing a draft, Spiral is your editor. Along with a built-in guardrails against AI-speak, you can set custom writing rules that Spiral applies in a “top edit,” the final expert-level edit on a piece—a term I learned working at Every.

We’ve written about the challenges of getting LLMs to write like you https://every.. It’s difficult to prompt an LLM to write like you, let alone get it to stop using common AI phrasing and punctuation. Spiral’s Style Engine is the best solution to this problem we’re aware of. An eval runs on every draft Spiral produces, challenging an LLM-as-judge to spot the generated draft among real samples in a blind lineup. Today we’re at 87 percent on this eval, meaning Spiral’s generated draft blends in with users’ samples almost nine times out of 10. When a draft is spotted, the judge explains why, creating a feedback loop to refine the Style Engine further.

Try Spiral 4.0 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW50LW5hdGl2ZSIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQyODgsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3dyaXRld2l0aHNwaXJhbC5jb20vP3NvdXJjZT1wb3N0X2J1dHRvbiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMn0=


SPIRAL GOES AGENT-NATIVE

As Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiU3BpcmFsIDQuMCBHb2VzIEFnZW

Caption: 

Together with

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/530680bc-0d72-4014-acc0-090de8866880/Wispr_Flow_Logo__2___1___1_.png?t=1774963064)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)
Caption: 



----------
==howdy, it’s Barsee again.==

==happy wednesday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.==

==**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**==

* NewLimit wants to test age-reversing medicine in humans

* OpenAI turns Codex into a website builder

* Microsoft unveils an always-on AI assistant

* Martin Scorsese backs AI startup for film storyboarding

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **WISPR FLOW**

## [Everywhere workflow (one voice layer across tools)](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/d47f0421-1c14-4847-832c-689a4c2a7d92/Newsletters_Image_1920x1080__7___5_.png?t=1780496729)
Follow image link: (https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)
Caption: Courtesy: Wispr Flow

Flow is a voice layer that works anywhere you type. Use it to respond in Slack, write docs, craft emails, or prompt an AI assistant without switching tools or copy/pasting. It's the fastest way to get high-quality text into your workflow, whether you're drafting, editing, or ideating.

[**Try Flow Free**](https://ref.wisprflow.ai/aivalley)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

##### 1/ NewLimit wants to test age-reversing medicine in humans

Youtube: 2026 NewLimit intro (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OgbjycCpqTo)

[NewLimit](https://www.newlimit.com/), the anti-aging startup co-founded by Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong, has raised [$435 million in a Series C](https://blog.newlimit.com/p/newlimit-raises-435m-led-by-founders) led by Founders Fund. The company says it plans to begin its first human clinical trial next year after discovering a therapy that can reverse the biological age of old human liver cells. NewLimit is focused on "cell reprogramming," an emerging field that aims to restore aged cells to a more youthful state rather than treating diseases one by one. In early studies, the company's liver therapy improved recovery from injury, dietary stress, and alcohol-related damage. If successful in humans, it could mark one of the first real-world tests of whether aging itself can be treated as a medical condition.

##### 2/ OpenAI turns Codex into a website builder

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/cd12bd0e-f912-4bfc-bbd9-f0b9934f2914/sites-list-light__1_.png?t=1780495849)
Caption: 

OpenAI has expanded Codex beyond software development with a new feature called [Sites](https://x.com/OpenAI/status/2061845949170045346?s=20), which lets users generate and publish websites and web apps directly from AI prompts. The company also introduced six industry-specific plug-ins for fields including sales, product design, data analytics, creative production, investment banking, and public equity investing. These tools package software and workflows into ready-to-use AI assistants that require little setup. OpenAI says more plug-ins are on the way, while partners including Wix, Replit, Figma, Webflow, and Lovable are helping build a broader Codex ecosystem. As Codex expands beyond coding, OpenAI is starting to compete directly with many of the software tools built on top of its own models.

##### 3/ Microsoft unveils an always-on AI assistant

Youtube: Microsoft Build event in 25 minutes (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gw0HBKJlX-w)

Microsoft used [Build 2026](https://news.microsoft.com/build-2026-live-blog) to unveil new AI models, an always-on assistant called [Scout](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/blog/2026/06/02/introducing-microsoft-scout-your-always-on-personal-agent/), and a new quantum chip. Scout can schedule meetings, prepare documents, and complete tasks across Microsoft's ecosystem without being directly prompted. The company also released [seven](https://x.com/MicrosoftAI/status/2061887500541366489?s=20) new in-house MAI models covering reasoning, coding, image generation, voice, and transcription. Microsoft also revealed [Majorana 2](https://news.microsoft.com/source/features/innovation/majorana-2-microsoft-discovery-agentic-ai/), which it says improves quantum reliability by 1,000x and could accelerate the path toward practical quantum computers. Together, the announcements show how aggressively Microsoft is positioning Windows and Microsoft 365 for a future built around AI agents.

##### 4/ Martin Scorsese backs AI startup for film storyboarding

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/668c3776-8dfa-4fa9-bffe-c3e7306c3209/scorcese-bfl__1_.png?t=1780495821)
Caption: 

Legendary filmmaker Martin Scorsese has [publicly joined](https://bfl.ai/martin-scorsese-bfl-advisor) AI image startup [Black Forest Labs](https://bfl.ai/) as an adviser, revealing that he is using the company's FLUX model to help storyboard a new film. Scorsese said the technology allows him to quickly visualize and share ideas during preproduction, calling the process "creatively freeing." He emphasized that the tool is only being used for storyboards, not to generate actors, sets, or finished scenes. The endorsement is notable given Hollywood's ongoing debate around AI, with Scorsese positioning the technology as a creative tool that supports filmmakers rather than replacing them.


----------

----------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Ollie](https://x.com/blennon_/status/2061868938443550842?s=20) > The world's first AI family assistant, designed to manage family life better than any human

* [Hermes Desktop](https://x.com/NousResearch/status/2061843507417944552?s=20) > Everything you love about Hermes, now running natively on your machine

* [Factory Router](https://x.com/FactoryAI/status/2061862733126275549?s=20) > Automatically selects the best AI model for every task

* [Devin Desktop](https://devin.ai/download) > A unified workspace for local and cloud coding agents, including Devin, Claude Code, Codex, and custom agents via the open Agent Client Protocol. Free to download today

* [Microsoft Scout](https://x.com/Microsoft365/status/2061874930547871868?s=20) > An always-on AI agent that keeps work moving by taking action without needing constant prompts

* Stack > The AI operating system that lets accounting firms take on more clients without hiring

* [Morpheus AI](https://about.roblox.com/newsroom/2026/04/roblox-reality-hybrid-architecture-democratizing-photorealistic-multiplayer-gaming) > Roblox acquires Morpheus AI, a startup building AI-powered video world models

* [Fundraisly](https://fundraisly.com/) > An AI fundraising agent that finds investors and books meetings on your behalf

* [Rodeo by TwelveLabs](https://tryrodeo.io/) > Describe your shot, and Rodeo creates the first cut automatically


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1ab2fe42-4d8f-400c-adc8-d2a2b14392cf/Screenshot_2026-06-03_at_2.04.59_PM__1_.png?t=1780494823)
Caption: 

* [ChatGPT reaches 1 billion monthly active users, the fast
